devdoc.details: Contract for managing patient data, medical records, and emergency access.

AccessControlBadConfirmation().details: The caller of a function is not the expected one. NOTE: Don't confuse with {AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount}.

ERC721IncorrectOwner(address,uint256,add.details: Indicates an error related to the ownership over a particular token. Used in transfers.

params.owner: Address of the current owner of a token.
params.sender: Address whose tokens are being transferred.

ERC721InsufficientApproval(address,uint2.details: Indicates a failure with the `operator`’s approval. Used in transfers.

params.operator: Address that may be allowed to operate on tokens without being their owner.

ERC721InvalidApprover(address).details: Indicates a failure with the `approver` of a token to be approved. Used in approvals.

ERC721InvalidOperator(address).details: Indicates a failure with the `operator` to be approved. Used in approvals.

params.operator: Address that may be allowed to operate on tokens without being their owner.

ERC721InvalidOwner(address).details: Indicates that an address can't be an owner. For example, `address(0)` is a forbidden owner in EIP-20. Used in balance queries.

params.owner: Address of the current owner of a token.

ERC721InvalidReceiver(address).details: Indicates a failure with the token `receiver`. Used in transfers.

params.receiver: Address to which tokens are being transferred.

ERC721InvalidSender(address).details: Indicates a failure with the token `sender`. Used in transfers.

params.sender: Address whose tokens are being transferred.

ERC721NonexistentToken(uint256).details: Indicates a `tokenId` whose `owner` is the zero address.

EnforcedPause().details: The operation failed because the contract is paused.

ExpectedPause().details: The operation failed because the contract is not paused.

Approval(address,address,uint256).details: Emitted when `owner` enables `approved` to manage the `tokenId` token.

ApprovalForAll(address,address,bool).details: Emitted when `owner` enables or disables (`approved`) `operator` to manage all of its assets.

BatchMetadataUpdate(uint256,uint256).details: This event emits when the metadata of a range of tokens is changed. So that the third-party platforms such as NFT market could timely update the images and related attributes of the NFTs.

params.accessor: The address that accessed the records

MetadataUpdate(uint256).details: This event emits when the metadata of a token is changed. So that the third-party platforms such as NFT market could timely update the images and related attributes of the NFT.

Paused(address).details: Emitted when the pause is triggered by `account`.

RoleAdminChanged(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32.details: Emitted when `newAdminRole` is set as ``role``'s admin role, replacing `previousAdminRole` `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is the starting admin for all roles, despite {RoleAdminChanged} not being emitted signaling this.

RoleGranted(bytes32,address,address).details: Emitted when `account` is granted `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call, an admin role bearer except when using {AccessControl-_setupRole}.

RoleRevoked(bytes32,address,address).details: Emitted when `account` is revoked `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call:   - if using `revokeRole`, it is the admin role bearer   - if using `renounceRole`, it is the role bearer (i.e. `account`)

Transfer(address,address,uint256).details: Emitted when `tokenId` token is transferred from `from` to `to`.

Unpaused(address).details: Emitted when the pause is lifted by `account`.

addMedicalRecord(string,string,string,st.details: Adds a medical record for the patient and mints an NFT.

params._ipfsHash: IPFS hash of the medical document.
params._isEmergencyViewable: Whether this record can be accessed in emergencies.

addNominee(string,address,string,string).details: Adds a nominee (emergency contact) for the patient.

constructor.details: Constructor that initializes the ERC721 token with name and symbol.

getAccessLogs(uint256).details: Step 2: Retrieves the audit trail (access logs) for a patient.

getMedicalRecords(uint256).details: Retrieves all medical records for a patient.

getNominees(uint256).details: Retrieves the list of nominees for a patient.

getPatientDetails(uint256).details: Retrieves detailed information for a specific patient.

returns._0: PatientDetails struct containing all patient information.
Patient ID or 0 if not found.

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

logEmergencyAccess(uint256,string).details: Step 1: Logs an emergency "Break-Glass" access event for audit trail.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

registerPatient(string,string,string,uin.details: Registers a new patient in the system.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

setEmergencyAccessHash(bytes32).details: Sets the emergency access hash for the calling patient.

params._hash: Hash generated for emergency magic link verification.

toggleEmergencyVisibility(uint256).details: Toggles the emergency visibility of a specific medical record.

params._recordIndex: Index of the medical record in the patient's array.

userExists(address).details: Checks if a wallet address belongs to a registered patient.

returns._0: True if the address belongs to a registered patient.

verifyEmergencyHash(uint256,bytes32).details: Verifies if a given hash matches the patient's emergency access hash.

_nextTokenId.details: Counter for generating unique NFT token IDs

patientIdCounter.details: Counter for assigning unique patient IDs

devdoc.title: Patient

EmergencyAccessLogged(uint256,address,st.notice: Emitted when emergency access is logged for audit purposes

RecordAdded(uint256,uint256,string).notice: Emitted when a medical record is added

addMedicalRecord(string,string,string,st.notice: Mints an ERC721 NFT representing the medical record. Caller must be registered.

addNominee(string,address,string,string).notice: Caller must be a registered patient. Multiple nominees allowed.

emergencyAccessHashes(uint256).notice: Mapping from patient ID to emergency access hash (for magic link verification)

emergencyAccessLogs(uint256,uint256).notice: Mapping from patient ID to emergency access logs

getAccessLogs(uint256).notice: Used for compliance and audit purposes.

getMedicalRecords(uint256).notice: Validates patient ID is within valid range.

getPatientDetails(uint256).notice: Validates patient ID is within valid range.

logEmergencyAccess(uint256,string).notice: Creates an immutable log of who accessed what and why.

patientNominees(uint256,uint256).notice: Mapping from patient ID to list of nominees

patients(uint256).notice: Mapping from patient ID to PatientDetails

registerPatient(string,string,string,uin.notice: Caller must not already be registered. Username must be unique.

setEmergencyAccessHash(bytes32).notice: Caller must be a registered patient.

toggleEmergencyVisibility(uint256).notice: Caller must be a registered patient. Toggles between viewable/non-viewable.

updatePatientDetails(string,string,uint8.notice: Caller must be a registered patient.

usernameToPatientId(string).notice: Mapping from username to patient ID (for friendly lookups)

walletToPatientId(address).notice: Mapping from wallet address to patient ID

userdoc.notice: Extends ERC721 for medical record NFTs. Patients control their data and can grant/revoke access.
